content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA

STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION
1020 N STREET, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA
(P.O. BOX 942879, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 94279-0001)

(916) 327-2217

WILLIAM M. BENNETT
First District, Kentfield

BRAD SHERMAN
Second District, Los Angeles

ERNEST J. DRONENBURG, JR.
Third District, San Diego

MATTHEW K. FONG
Fourth District, Los Angeles

GRAY DAVIS
Controller, Sacramento

October 21, 1991

CINDY RAMBO
Executive Director

Mr. Ben Davidian
Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Attention: Ms. Margaret Ellison, Counsel

Dear Mr. Davidian:

Until the passage of Proposition 73 in June 1988, the
Board of Equalization's Annual Report included pictures of each
Board Member, a biography, and a letter signed by the current
Chairman. Because of uncertainty over the correct application of
Proposition 73, the pictures, letter, and biographies were
deleted. The Board of Equalization is now preparing its 1990-91
Annual Report and plans to include pictures, a signed letter from
the Chairman, and biographies of the individual Board Members.
The purpose of this letter is to request a formal opinion from
the Commission regarding the correct application of Fair
Political Practices Regulation 18901 (2 Cal. Code Regs. 18901) as
it relates to mailings by the individual Members of the Board of
Equalization. Our questions are as follows:

Question I

The Board of Equalization mails approximately 4,000
copies of its Annual Report. (See attached chart for specific
breakdown.) Of these 4,000 copies, only 370 copies would be
considered to meet the criteria listed in Regulation 18901(b) (we
note that the approximately 370 copies supplied to Board
Members' offices, none have been "mass mailed" within the meaning
of Regulation 18901(b)). The 370 copies of the Annual Report
currently supplied to the Board Members are without any
conditions or monitoring by the agency. There is no plan or
scheme for further distribution and no information is given by
the Board Members to the agency as to how the Annual Reports are
distributed. If no more than 199 copies of these 370 are mailed
in a single calendar month, does Regulation 18901 apply?


Mr. Ben Davidian                    -2-                    October 21, 1991


Question II

     The State Board of Equalization is composed of four
elected members, each representing a separate equalization
district of over 7 million Californians, and by one ex-officio
member, the State Controller, who is elected statewide.  Each
Board Member has broad authority within his or her particular
equalization district (see general Gov. Code § 15623).  The
individual Board Members are prohibited from acting as a Board or
in a concerted manner unless it is at a duly noticed public
meeting (Gov. Code §§ 11125 et seq.).

     Currently, individual legislators are not all grouped
together and treated in the aggregate nor are they required to
coordinate their mailings with the other legislators or a central
office.  Legislators may each mail up to 199 substantially
similar pieces in a single calendar month without such a mailing
falling within the prohibitions of Regulation 18901.  An example
of this is the annual printing and mailing of the California
State Constitution by members of the Assembly wherein legislators
may send up to 199 copies a month.  Does the fact that each Board
Member is an elected constitutional officer representing separate
districts require that they be treated separately for purposes of
determining whether a "mass mailing" under Regulation 18901 has
occurred and may each Board Member independently distribute 199
copies or less of the same item each month without violating
Regulation 18901?

     If you or your staff wish to clarify any of these
questions, please call or write the Board's Chief Counsel,
Mr. F. L. Sorensen, Jr., at 445-4380 or Ms. Mary C. Armstrong at
324-5589.  Thank you for your attention to this matter.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Burton W. Oliver
                                        Executive Director

BWO:wk
3324C
Attachment
cc:  Honorable Brad Sherman
     Honorable Ernest J. Dronenburg, Jr.
     Honorable William Bennett
     Honorable Matthew K. Fong
     Honorable Gray Davis


ATTACHMENT 1

Annual Report Distribution
(1990)

AREA AT ISSUE:

Board Member Offices

(All four Elected Board Members) . . . . 370

Exemption per
Reg. 18901(b):

Board Excise Tax Unit............................. 75    (b)(4)
Board District Offices (Statewide).............. 600    (b)(4)
Board Headquarters (Sacramento Area)............ 266    (b)(4)
City Clerks.......................................... 406    (b)(3)
County Assessors..................................... 58    (b)(3)
County Auditors...................................... 58    (b)(3)
Legislators.......................................... 26    (b)(3)
Unsolicited Requests received by January 1.... 500    (a)(4)
Unsolicited Requests received during the year..1,000    (a)(4)

TOTAL...............................................2,989

sections.question:
I

The Board of Equalization mails approximately 4,000 copies of its Annual Report. (See attached chart for specific breakdown.) Of these 4,000 copies, only 370 copies would be considered to meet the criteria listed in Regulation 18901(b) (we note that of the approximately 370 copies supplied to Board Members' offices, none have been "mass mailed" within the meaning of Regulation 18901(b)). The 370 copies of the Annual Report currently supplied to the Board Members are without any conditions or monitoring by the agency. There is no plan or scheme for further distribution and no information is given by the Board Members to the agency as to how the Annual Reports are distributed. If no more than 199 copies of these 370 are mailed in a single calendar month, does Regulation 18901 apply?

The image appears to be extremely faint and mostly illegible, with very little visible content that can be reliably transcribed or described. If you have another scan or higher quality image, please provide it for better results.

Question II

The State Board of Equalization is composed of four elected members, each representing a separate equalization district of over 7 million Californians, and by one ex-officio member, the State Controller, who is elected statewide. Each Board Member has broad authority within his or her particular equalization district (see general Gov. Code § 15623). The individual Board Members are prohibited from acting as a Board or in a concerted manner unless it is at a duly noticed public meeting (Gov. Code §§ 11125 et seq.).

Currently, individual legislators are not all grouped together and treated in the aggregate nor are they required to coordinate their mailings with the other legislators or a central office. Legislators may each mail up to 199 substantially similar pieces in a single calendar month without such a mailing falling within the prohibitions of Regulation 18901. An example of this is the annual printing and mailing of the California State Constitution by members of the Assembly wherein legislators may send up to 199 copies a month. Does the fact that each Board Member is an elected constitutional officer representing separate districts require that they be treated separately for purposes of determining whether a "mass mailing" under Regulation 18901 has occurred and may each Board Member independently distribute 199 copies or less of the same item each month without violating Regulation 18901?

If you or your staff wish to clarify any of these questions, please call or write the Board's Chief Counsel, Mr. E. L. Sorensen, Jr., at 445-4380 or Ms. Mary C. Armstrong at 324-5589. Thank you for your attention to this matter.
